intro: The clearest split is that Ably has a free tier while Pusher does not. Ably is Real-time messaging infrastructure with pub/sub, presence, and 99.999% uptime SLA across global PoPs; Pusher is Real-time messaging platform with Channels for pub/sub and Beams for push notifications. The data points Ably at chat applications and live collaboration features, and Pusher at chat applications and live collaboration features. Pricing: Ably is subscription from $29 / month; Pusher is seat. Lock-in is low for Ably and medium for Pusher, with DX at 5/5 vs 5/5 and transparency at 4/5 vs 3/5. On listed strengths, Ably emphasises chat applications and live collaboration features, and Pusher emphasises chat applications and live collaboration features. Both offer an enterprise tier for larger rollouts. Honest trade-off: neither fits static projects, so if that is your context, look elsewhere; otherwise pick on the pivotal factor above and the DX score that matters most to your team.
quick_take: Ably is for chat applications; Pusher is for chat applications; decide based on whether a free tier matters.
verdict_a: Choose Ably if you want to start on a free tier before paying and your workload matches chat applications. Skip it if your context is static projects.
verdict_b: Choose Pusher if starting on a paid subscription upfront is acceptable and your workload matches chat applications. Skip it if your context is static projects.

faq.a: Entry pricing is listed as $29 / month for Ably and unknown for Pusher. Ably offers a free tier while Pusher does not, so initial cost favors Ably. Total cost at scale depends on usage and plan details that are not in this dataset, so confirm with each vendor.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Ably to Pusher?
faq.a: Ably is tagged with low lock-in (proprietary pub/sub protocol and presence model). Pusher is tagged with medium lock-in (proprietary pub/sub, swap to ably possible). Plan for SDK replacement, data export, and a parallel-run period; the dataset does not include migration tooling details.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Ably or Pusher?
faq.a: Both are rated 5/5 for developer experience in this dataset. With equal scores, the choice comes down to the specific SDK surface and docs you will use day to day. Run a short spike on each before deciding.

faq.q: Is Pusher a good alternative to Ably?
faq.a: Yes for teams whose workload matches chat applications, since both list the same fit. Pusher is flagged as not for static projects, so confirm your context does not fall there. Note that moving from Ably to Pusher means leaving a free tier behind.
